name: Scientific simulation / HPC application
summary: A compute-intensive application that numerically solves scientific/engineering models over large datasets, typically as parallel batch jobs on clusters or supercomputers. Correctness is a matter of numerical accuracy and validation against reference/experimental results, and value is dominated by parallel-scaling efficiency within a finite compute allocation.

concerns: numerical accuracy: required precision, error bounds, convergence criteria, and tolerance on residuals
verification & validation (V&V): verification against analytic/method-of-manufactured-solutions, validation against experimental/reference data
numerical stability, conditioning, and controlled error propagation across the solution
the physical/mathematical model, its assumptions, boundary/initial conditions, and its domain of validity
uncertainty quantification and sensitivity of outputs to inputs and discretisation
questions: Are required numerical accuracy, error bounds, and convergence criteria quantified?
Is a verification & validation plan defined against analytic or experimental reference results?
Are the model's assumptions, boundary conditions, and domain of validity stated?

concerns: parallel scaling efficiency: strong- and weak-scaling targets and the node/core count they hold to
arithmetic throughput (FLOPS) vs memory-bandwidth / communication bound; roofline positioning
load balancing across ranks/threads and minimisation of MPI communication and synchronisation overhead
I/O performance for large reads/writes (parallel file systems, collective I/O)
accelerator (GPU) utilisation, host-device transfer overhead, and memory-hierarchy efficiency
questions: Are strong- and weak-scaling efficiency targets specified with the core/node counts they hold at?
Are communication, load-balance, and I/O throughput bounds defined for the target scale?
Is target accelerator utilisation and memory-bandwidth efficiency stated?

concerns: checkpoint/restart to survive node failures and walltime limits on long-running jobs
fault tolerance at scale where the aggregate MTBF of thousands of nodes is short relative to runtime
detection and handling of silent data corruption and non-convergence / solver divergence
recoverability without loss of days of compute; restart consistency and determinism of resumed runs
resource-exhaustion behaviour (out-of-memory, disk-full, walltime kill)
questions: Is checkpoint/restart required, with a defined interval balancing overhead against expected node MTBF?
Is behaviour on solver divergence, silent corruption, or resource exhaustion specified?
Is a resumed run required to be consistent (and, where needed, bit-reproducible)?

concerns: very large input/output datasets: scientific formats (HDF5, NetCDF, ADIOS) and their schemas
output data volume, in-situ processing / reduction, and retention of raw vs derived results
provenance/metadata linking results to code version, inputs, parameters, and hardware for reproducibility
data staging between long-term storage, parallel scratch, and node-local storage
data integrity (checksums) for petabyte-scale transfers and archives
questions: Are the scientific data formats, schemas, and expected output volumes specified?
Is provenance metadata (code version, inputs, parameters) captured to make results reproducible?
Are data staging, retention, and integrity requirements defined for the dataset scale?

concerns: reproducibility and determinism: pinned compilers, libraries (BLAS/LAPACK/MPI), flags, and controlled floating-point behaviour
bit-for-bit vs statistical reproducibility policy across runs, core counts, and platforms
regression testing of numerical results (tolerance-based comparison, not exact equality)
modularity separating physics/model, numerics, and parallel infrastructure
verification test suites (MMS, conservation checks) integrated into CI
questions: Is the required level of reproducibility (bit-exact vs within-tolerance) stated across platforms and core counts?
Are the toolchain, libraries, and floating-point settings pinned for reproducible builds?
Are tolerance-based numerical regression tests part of the verification suite?

concerns: batch scheduler integration (Slurm/PBS/LSF): job scripts, queues, partitions, and dependencies
resource requests and limits (nodes, cores, GPUs, memory, walltime) and fit to queue policy
allocation/quota accounting and fair-share consumption within a compute grant
job monitoring, failure detection, and automatic resubmission of preempted/failed jobs
environment/module management on the target cluster (modules, containers, Spack/EasyBuild)
questions: Are scheduler resource requests (nodes, GPUs, memory, walltime) and queue constraints specified?
Is allocation/quota consumption accounted for against the compute grant?
Is automatic detection and resubmission of failed/preempted jobs defined?

concerns: portability across HPC platforms, CPU/GPU architectures, and interconnects without correctness change
performance portability: acceptable efficiency across vendors (e.g. via Kokkos/SYCL/OpenMP-offload) vs vendor-locked kernels
build/packaging on target clusters (Spack, EasyBuild, containers such as Apptainer/Singularity)
dependence on specific vendor libraries, compilers, or MPI implementations
questions: Are the target platforms, CPU/GPU architectures, and interconnects the code must run on specified?
Is a performance-portability expectation stated, or is vendor lock-in acceptable?
Is the build/packaging path for target clusters (Spack/containers) defined?

concerns: finite compute allocation / node-hour budget as a hard project constraint
licensing of commercial solvers, compilers, or libraries and concurrent-license limits
mandated facility software stack, MPI/compiler versions, and container policy
storage quota on scratch and long-term filesystems
questions: Is the compute allocation (node-hour) budget captured as a constraint on runs?
Are commercial-solver/library licensing and concurrency limits accounted for?
Are mandated facility software versions and storage quotas specified?
